data.title: Warner Music GAAP EPS of $0.21 misses by $0.13, revenue of $1.87B beats by $190M
data.description:
• Warner Music press release (WMG): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.21 misses by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $1.87B (+14.7% Y/Y) beats by $190M.
  • Digital revenue increased 8%, or 6% in constant currency.
  • Adjusted OIBDA increased 15% to $405 million versus $353 million in prior-year quarter (or 12% in constant currency)
  • Cash provided by operating activities decreased 24% to $231 million from $304 million in prior-year quarter

data.title: Nexxen seeks authorization for $40M ordinary share repurchase program
data.description:
• Nexxen International (NEXN) is seeking authorization to initiate a new ordinary share repurchase program for up to $40 million, which would commence following the completion of its current program.
  • The program’s commencement is contingent upon receiving consent from the company’s bank lenders. As of October 31, 2025, Nexxen had 57,086,122 ordinary shares outstanding (excluding treasury shares) and approximately $13.9 million remaining under its current share repurchase authorization.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia is 'firing on all cylinders' as it put a damper on nearly every bearish thesis
data.description:
The latest quarterly results and guidance from Nvidia (NVDA) were not just another shot in the proverbial arm for the artificial intelligence trade, Wall Street analysts said.

They're indicative that Nvidia is “firing on all cylinders” and the Jensen Huang-led company had a counterpunch for nearly every bearish take on the AI spending boom.

“It's a positive that Jensen [Huang] was willing to address AI bubble and circular financing concerns directly, while Nvidia's place in the ecosystem puts it in a difficult position to speak to those concerns, we think its explanations for wanting to enable the growth in the ecosystem, the useful lives of its products, and ultimately the value proposition for AI investments resonate,” Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore wrote in a note to clients. “That input is supportive, but as we forecast over $300B in revenue in FY27 confident that those investing the money will see [net present value of more than $300B] will have to come from other sources and some of those sources are not as definitive as what we heard today.” 

Moore, who has an Overweight rating and $235 price target on Nvidia, added that the “enthusiasm” from Nvidia's management for the opportunity still ahead is notable.

CEO Huang and others reiterated that there will be between $3T and $4T in annual AI infrastructure spending by the end of the decade. And with Nvidia having visibility of $500B in Blackwell and Rubin sales through 2026, and recent deals — such as the one with Anthropic (ANTHRO) not included in that figure, estimates are likely to go higher, Moore said.

“The Anthropic deal alone represents [$30B or more] in potential revenue to Nvidia, so while Nvidia's 10bn q/q growth is impressive, it continues to be the case that demand is growing much faster,” Moore added. “As long as that’s the case, there should be an upward bias on estimates.”

Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis pointed out that Nvidia “answered the bell,” amid the recent crop up in concerns related to AI demand, financing, and GPU lifespan and deprecation.

“We don't expect every AI bear to be satisfied, but these results and added context from management around demand outlook should offer some near-term reprieve,” Curtis wrote in a note to clients. “GB300 sales were very strong, accounting for 2/3s of Blackwell sales. [Gross margins] expected to be in the mid 70s [(versus Street at 74%)] despite concerns over rising input costs including memory. Commentary around [cloud service providers] being sold out across the board and full utilization for Blackwell, Hopper, and even Ampere should help put the useful life conversation to bed.” Curtis reiterated his Buy rating and raised his price target to $250 from $240.

Evercore ISI analyst Mark Lipacis reiterated that Nvidia is a Top Pick for the firm and despite the increased competition from AMD (AMD), Broadcom (AVGO) and others, Nvidia is still the “AI ecosystem of choice.”

“While supply- constraints remain, we believe the +32% [quarter-over-quarter] increase in inventory and +63% QQ increase in supply commitments positions NVDA to meet if not beat its $500bn bogey for DC compute orders in 2025+2026,” Lipacis wrote. “We believe risk/reward remains attractive and NVDA remains our Top Pick,” he added.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives, who has called Nvidia's Huang the “Godfather of AI,” added that the earnings call was “heard round the world.” 

“The pure Nvidia numbers/guidance and strategic vision shows the AI Revolution is NOT a Bubble, instead it's Year 3 of a 10-year build out of this 4th Industrial Revolution in our view,” Ives wrote. “That said, bears have missed every transformational tech name the last few decades worried about valuation and will miss these next few years of the tech bull market while hibernating in their caves. As someone like myself that spends so much time globally seeing the AI Revolution play out and gauging demand in the Asia supply chain, we believe this is still the Top of the 3rd inning in the AI Field of Dreams game being played.”

data.title: MAXIMUS misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• MAXIMUS press release (MMS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.62 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $1.32B (+0.8% Y/Y) misses by $20M.
  • A quarterly cash dividend of $0.30 per share payable on December 1, 2025, to shareholders of record on November 14, 2025.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Outlook

    Maximus is establishing fiscal year 2026 guidance. We expect revenue to range between $5.225 billion and $5.425 billion vs. $5.57B consensus. We anticipate an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 13.7% for the full fiscal year. We expect adjusted diluted earnings per share, which excludes expense for amortization of intangible assets and divestiture-related charges, to range between $7.95 and $8.25 per share vs. $7.63 consensus.

    We expect free cash flow to range between $450 million and $500 million for fiscal year 2026. We forecast expenses of $81 million for amortization of intangible assets, $58 million for depreciation and amortization, and $69 million for interest is anticipated for the full fiscal year. We expect an effective income tax rate of approximately 25% and weighted average shares outstanding of about 55.5 million is expected for fiscal year 2026.

data.title: ZIM Integrated beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ZIM Integrated press release (ZIM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.02 beats by $0.25.
  • Revenue of $1.78B (-35.7% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA1 of $593 Million and Adjusted EBIT1 of $260 Million.
  • Updated Full-Year 2025 Guidance The Company has updated its full year 2025 guidance and now expects to generate Adjusted EBITDA between $2.0 billion and $2.2 billion and Adjusted EBIT between $700 million and $900 million. Previously, the Company expected to generate Adjusted EBITDA between $1.8 billion and $2.2 billion and Adjusted EBIT between $550 million and $950 million.

data.title: Siemens Energy hits all-time high after unveiling €6B share buyback plan
data.description:
Siemens Energy (OTCPK:SMEGF) +5% to a new all-time high in European trading Thursday after saying it plans to buy back up to €6 billion ($6.9 billion) worth of shares over the next three years.

Siemens Energy's (OTCPK:SMEGF) stock has more than doubled YTD over optimism around surging data center investments, and said last week it plans to pay its first dividend in four years.

The company also cited strong global demand for gas turbines and energy grids in raising its medium-term financial targets, now forecasting a profit margin before special items in FY 2028 of 14%-16% compared with a previous target of 10%-12%, and compound annual revenue growth in the low-teens percentage range, above a prior target of high single-digit to low double-digit growth.

In its fiscal Q4 ending in September, Siemens Energy's (OTCPK:SMEGF) revenues rose 9.7% to €10.4 billion, and profit at the grid technologies unit, which supplies data centers, jumped 71%, giving a return before special items of nearly 15%.

data.title: Fossil gains as CEO Franco Fogliato buys 200,000 company shares
data.description:
• Shares of U.S. watchmaker Fossil (FOSL) rose 12% in premarket trading on Thursday after CEO Fogliato Franco purchased 200,000 shares of the company at $1.75–$1.82. 
  • Following the recent trade, Fogliato now owns 1,950,000 shares of the stock.

data.title: Bayer wins FDA accelerated approval for Hyrnuo in lung cancer
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) announced on Thursday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) approved its tyrosine kinase inhibitor Hyrnuo (sevabertinib) under the agency’s accelerated approval process for a type of lung cancer.

Accordingly, the oral small molecule drug will be available in the U.S. as a late-line option for adults with non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer whose tumors harbor certain genetic variations known as HER2 tyrosine kinase domain activating mutations.

The approval is based on data related to the objective response rate ((ORR)) and duration of response ((DOR)) in the company’s ongoing Phase 1/2 SOHO-01 trial for advanced NSCLC harboring a HER2-activating mutation.

Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) will be required to prove the treatment’s benefit in a confirmatory trial to ensure the continuation of the approval.

In May, the FDA granted priority review for the German conglomerate’s regulatory application for sevabertinib, making a potential approval the first global commercial indication of the antitumor agent.

data.title: Casino sector M&A: SJM Holdings acquires L'Arc Hotel in Macau
data.description:
SJM Holdings (OTCPK:SJMHY) (OTCPK:SJMHF) announced on Thursday the acquisition of Arc of Triumph Development Company Limited, which is the entity that owns the L’Arc Hotel in Macau. The deal price was HKD$1.75 billion ($225 million) and involved SJM’s (OTCPK:SJMHY) (OTCPK:SJMHF) subsidiaries purchasing the hotel from Goldarch Holdings and Solar King Investments, consolidating L’Arc Hotel as part of a core peninsula cluster of properties. 

L’Arc Hotel, which has historically operated under a service arrangement with SJM (OTCPK:SJMHY) (OTCPK:SJMHF) and served as a venue for SJM’s satellite casino operations, will now be a wholly owned asset within SJM’s portfolio.

The acquisition was highlighted as aligning with SJM's (OTCPK:SJMHY) (OTCPK:SJMHF) strategy to directly own and operate its key properties as Macau’s satellite casino legislative framework evolves. The move also allows the casino operator to streamline its operations and ensure compliance with new regulations. The service agreement previously governing the casino operations at L’Arc Hotel is set to end, meaning future gaming activities in the property will be fully integrated under direct management.

The casino company also announced that following a comprehensive business review, it will not proceed with the previously stated intention to acquire the property where Casino Ponte 16 is located. The decision was noted to be reached after a thorough assessment of long-term business planning, commercial considerations, and resource prioritization across the SJM (OTCPK:SJMHF) portfolio. The outcome was said to reflect the company’s disciplined approach to capital deployment and its focus on strengthening core operations in line with market developments and long term development strategy.

Shares of SJM Holdings (OTCPK:SJMHY) (OTCPK:SJMHF) were up 1.1% in Hong Kong trading.

data.title: Walmart beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Walmart press release (WMT): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.62 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $179.5B (+5.8% Y/Y) beats by $4.33B.
  • Globally, eCommerce grew 27% with growth in each business segment of more than 20%. Walmart U.S. comp sales2 up 4.5%, with strength across categories.
  • Q426: Revenue: $186.87B consensus vs. estimated growth of 4.50% Y/Y. Non-GAAP EPS: $0.73 consensus.
  • FY26: Revenue: $701.24B consensus vs. estimated growth of 3.96% Y/Y (prior increase 3.75% to 4.75%). Non-GAAP EPS: $2.61 consensus (prior $2.52 to $2.62).
  • For fiscal year 2026, the Company raises outlook for growth in net sales to 4.8% to 5.1% and adjusted operating income to 4.8% to 5.5%, both in constant currency (“cc”)1 . Adjusted EPS1 is expected to be $2.58 to $2.63, including a currency headwind of $0.01 to $0.02
  • Shares -2.46% PM.
